Package application

Class ViewManager

java.lang.Object
application.ViewManager
public class ViewManager extends Object
The ViewManager coordinates all navigation and visual transitions within the application.

Instead of traditional JavaFX scene switching, this manager swaps the contents of a primary StackPane. This allows for complex transitions (Fade, Zoom) and the maintenance of a global "Shader" layer (e.g., GlowOverlay) that persists across different views.

  • Nested Class Summary

    class 
    ViewManager.ViewTransition
    Inner class responsible for executing JavaFX animations during view changes.

  • Method Details

    • getInstance

      public static ViewManager getInstance()
      Returns:
      The singleton instance of the ViewManager.

    • init

      public static void init(javafx.stage.Stage stage, View initialView)
      Bootstraps the ViewManager with the primary stage.
      Parameters:
      stage - The JavaFX Stage.
      initialView - The first view to display (usually MainMenu).

    • switchView

      public void switchView(View newView, TransitionType transitionType)
      Navigates to a new view and pushes it onto the history stack.
      Parameters:
      newView - The view to transition to.
      transitionType - The animation style.

    • switchViewReplace

      public void switchViewReplace(View newView, TransitionType transitionType)
      Replaces the current view with a new one without growing the stack. Useful for linear progression (e.g., Level 1 -> Level 2).

    • switchToPreviousView

      public boolean switchToPreviousView(TransitionType transitionType)
      Pops the current view and returns to the previous one in the stack.
      Returns:
      true if navigation was possible.

    • getCurrentView

      public View getCurrentView()
      Returns:
      The view at the top of the stack.

    • currentViewIs

      public boolean currentViewIs(Class<? extends View> viewClass)
      Checks if the current view is an instance of a specific class.
